title: Reimplement mechanical scan phases (secret-leak, flake-rerun) as native Go in-process phases — zero REPL boots, zero tokens, milliseconds instead of minutes
summary: OPERATOR-BOOSTED 2026-07-13 ("move those tasks up in the priority now"). Rule: LLM cycles are for judgment; deterministic work belongs in code. Two goal-recipe scan phases are fundamentally deterministic yet run as full REPL-booted LLM phases today: (1) secret-leak-scan = pattern scanning over the cycle diff (gitleaks-class regex corpus + entropy heuristics) — measured 0-2K output/run but a full boot (~30-60s + instruction tax) every time; (2) flake-rerun-scan = re-run the failed/new tests N times and diff outcomes — measured 5.5K output/run on balanced tier (costs like a build!) for work that is exec+compare. Reimplement both as native phase implementations behind the existing in-process seam (evolve phase / phaseproto serve-phase; ship is already native precedent), emitting the SAME deliverable contract artifacts (report md + verdict) so the spine/contract gates are untouched. Keep error-handling-scan and adversarial-review as LLM phases (genuine judgment). Expected saving at current recipe frequency: ~2 boots + ~6-8K output tokens + ~10-15 min wall per cycle that inserts both scans. Wire a policy escape hatch (phase impl: native|llm) via the registry phase spec, NOT an env flag.
acceptance: RED: registry phase spec selects impl per phase; llm impl remains available (no behavior flag envs)
batch soak: scan phases show 0 tokens + no bridge sessions; go test -race PASS; apicover clean (graduate any new package!)
source: tokens report baseline 2026-07-13 (operator ultrathink); Rule 5 deterministic-work-in-code
